task_completion.description: The OpenBCI GUI dashboard is configured with a 6-panel layout.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The main workspace of the OpenBCI GUI is divided into exactly 6 distinct widget panels arranged in a grid.

task_completion.description: Two separate Band Power widgets are visible in the dashboard.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Two distinct panels each display frequency-band bar charts with Delta/Theta/Alpha/Beta/Gamma labels. Both are labeled 'Band Power'.

task_completion.description: One Band Power widget shows only left-hemisphere channels (CH1, CH3, CH5, CH7).
task_completion.visual_evidence: One of the Band Power widgets shows bars for exactly 4 channels. The channel toggles or legend show only odd-numbered channels (1, 3, 5, 7) as active, with even channels (2, 4, 6, 8) deselected or absent.

task_completion.description: The other Band Power widget shows only right-hemisphere channels (CH2, CH4, CH6, CH8).
task_completion.visual_evidence: The other Band Power widget shows bars for exactly 4 channels. The channel toggles or legend show only even-numbered channels (2, 4, 6, 8) as active, with odd channels (1, 3, 5, 7) deselected or absent.

task_completion.description: An FFT Plot widget is visible in one of the 6 panels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: One panel shows a frequency spectrum plot with colored lines representing channel spectra, labeled 'FFT Plot'.

task_completion.description: A Head Plot widget is visible in one of the 6 panels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: One panel shows a circular topographic head map with colored regions representing EEG amplitude distribution, labeled 'Head Plot'.

task_completion.description: A Networking widget is visible in one of the 6 panels.
task_completion.visual_evidence: One panel shows the Networking widget with protocol dropdowns, stream configuration fields, and start/stop buttons.

task_completion.description: The bandpass filter is set to 1-40 Hz.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The filter controls or status indicators show bandpass range of 1 Hz to 40 Hz.

task_completion.description: The notch filter is set to 60 Hz.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The filter controls show the notch filter set to '60 Hz'.

task_completion.description: The Networking widget shows an LSL stream configured with BandPower data and the name 'Asymmetry_Monitor'.
task_completion.visual_evidence: In the Networking widget, the protocol dropdown shows 'LSL', the data type shows 'BandPower', and the stream name field contains 'Asymmetry_Monitor'.

task_completion.description: EEG data is actively streaming with live waveforms visible.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The Time Series widget shows active scrolling EEG waveforms and the control bar shows 'Stop Data Stream' (indicating data is flowing).

task_completion.description: A recording of at least 15 seconds of data was captured.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Evidence that the agent clicked a Record button and waited before stopping, or a terminal/file manager shows a new recording file in the Recordings directory.

task_completion.description: A configuration report was written to ~/Documents/asymmetry_station_report.txt via terminal.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window shows commands writing to asymmetry_station_report.txt, with content listing left-hemisphere channels (CH1/CH3/CH5/CH7), right-hemisphere channels (CH2/CH4/CH6/CH8), filter settings (1-40 Hz bandpass, 60 Hz notch), and the LSL stream name 'Asymmetry_Monitor'.

integrity.description: The agent interacted with the actual OpenBCI GUI to configure widgets, not by editing configuration files in a text editor.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots show the graphical OpenBCI GUI being manipulated through mouse clicks on widget dropdowns, channel toggles, and filter controls.

integrity.description: Genuine EEG waveforms are visible and updating in the Time Series widget, confirming the Synthetic session is active.
integrity.visual_evidence: Time Series panel shows non-static, non-flatlined waveform traces across channels.

integrity.description: The agent did not bypass GUI configuration by directly writing JSON settings files from the terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: The terminal should not show commands that create or edit OpenBCI settings JSON files directly to fake the configuration.

privileged_info_for_vlm: OpenBCI GUI v5.2.2 running a SYNTHETIC Cyton 8-channel session. The 6-panel layout shows widgets in a 3x2 or 2x3 grid. Band Power widgets display colored bar charts for each frequency band (Delta/Theta/Alpha/Beta/Gamma). In OpenBCI GUI, channel colors follow a fixed palette: CH1=Grey, CH2=Purple, CH3=Light Blue, CH4=Green, CH5=Yellow, CH6=Orange, CH7=Red, CH8=Dark Red. Left hemisphere (odd channels: 1,3,5,7 = Fp1,C3,P7,O1) should show Grey, Light Blue, Yellow, Red bars. Right hemisphere (even channels: 2,4,6,8 = Fp2,C4,P8,O2) should show Purple, Green, Orange, Dark Red bars. The Networking widget supports LSL/UDP/OSC/Serial protocols with up to 4 streams. The bandpass filter is accessible via the Filters button or dropdown in the toolbar. Expert Mode is toggled via the top navigation bar.
